title: smilacaceae
intro: The Smilacaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the sarsaparilla or greenbrier family. This family consists of around 30-40 species of flowering plants, primarily found in tropical and temperate regions of the world. Smilacaceae members are known for their twining or climbing habits, and often produce showy white or purple flowers that are followed by red or purple berries. They are also notable for their use in traditional medicine, with some species containing compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
